title: Cyathea latebrosa
taxonomy: Cyathea latebrosa is classified as a species of fern in the family Cyatheaceae. It belongs to the genus Cyathea, which is a group of tree ferns. The species is native to New Zealand and is known for its distinctive fronds. Cyathea latebrosa is often found growing in shaded, moist areas, such as forests and woodland edges. The classification of Cyathea latebrosa reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
